items.reason: Removed the obsolete DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT client environment variable entry after sampling config became worker-authoritative.
Replaced the old log_rate_limit quota section with request/access/diagnostic sample-rate behavior, derivation, explicit 1.0 semantics, and configuration paths.
Updated the dscli logging configuration table from log_rate_limit to request_sample_rate, access_sample_rate, and diagnostic_sample_rate with derivation notes.
Added routing entries for the new log-sampler design context.
Updated the logging module overview to include LogSampler responsibilities and route readers to the new sampler design.
Added a source-backed design document for the new class-based random sampling behavior, config propagation, and hot-path constraints.
Still documents log_rate_limit, LogRateLimiter, DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT, and register-time log_rate_limit sync instead of LogSampler and LogSampleConfigPb.
Still says SetRequestTraceUUID creates decisions when log_rate_limit is enabled and that LogRateLimiter owns the per-second admission counter.
Still frames PLOG slow diagnostics as bypassing log_rate_limit rather than the new request_sample_rate plus diagnostic sampling semantics.
Still summarizes PLOG slow Get as bypassing log_rate_limit and LogRateLimiter, which is stale after the sampler replacement.
